mission.title: Install and Configure VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid
mission.description: VMware Tanzu Kubernetes Grid is a distribution that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters in an enterprise environment. It leverages the Tanzu Framework and Carvel packaging to provide a cohesive experience for managing Kubernetes workloads. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with VMware tools and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Install the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Tanzu CLI, which is essential for managing Tanzu Kubernetes Grid.

```bash

curl -L https://github.com/vmware-tanzu/tanzu-framework/releases/download/v0.29.0/tanzu-framework-linux-amd64.tar.gz | tar xz -C /usr/local/bin

```

> This command downloads and extracts the Tanzu CLI to the local bin directory.

steps.title: Initialize the Tanzu CLI
steps.description:
This step initializes the Tanzu CLI and sets up the required plugins.

```bash

tanzu init

```

> This command initializes the Tanzu CLI and prepares it for use.

steps.title: Create a Tanzu Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Tanzu Kubernetes cluster with specified parameters.

```bash

tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --node-count 3 --region us-west-2

```

> Replace 'my-cluster' with your desired cluster name.
commands: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --node-count 3 --region us-west-2

steps.title: Configure Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to access the newly created cluster.

```bash

tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context

```

> This command sets the kubeconfig context to the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

uninstall.title: Delete the Tanzu Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step removes the cluster and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

tanzu cluster delete my-cluster

```

> This will delete the cluster and all associated resources. Ensure you have backups of any important data.

uninstall.title: Remove the Tanzu CLI
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Tanzu CLI from your system.

```bash

rm /usr/local/bin/tanzu

```

> This command deletes the Tanzu CLI binary.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Tanzu Kubernetes Grid
upgrade.description:
Backup your cluster, drain nodes if necessary, perform the upgrade, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

tanzu cluster backup my-cluster

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

tanzu cluster upgrade my-cluster --kubernetes-version v1.29.0

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup your cluster before upgrading.
> Drain each node before upgrading.
> Upgrade the cluster to the specified Kubernetes version.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' status after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient permissions or misconfigured settings.

**Fix:**
Ensure you have the necessary permissions and that your configuration settings are correct.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubeconfig context not set'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the kubeconfig file is not properly configured.

**Fix:**
Run 'tanzu cluster kubeconfig get my-cluster --admin --context' to set the context.

troubleshooting.title: Nodes are in 'NotReady' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the node is not properly initialized or has network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the node logs using 'kubectl describe node <node-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize existing nodes to meet resource requirements.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes v1.29, improved CLI commands for cluster management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy commands related to cluster creation.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 2.4, ensure to review the new CLI commands and adjust your scripts accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' state.
codeSnippets: tanzu cluster create my-cluster --plan dev --kubernetes-version v1.29.0 --node-count 3 --region us-west-2

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
